title: The 2025-26 Boston Bruins exceeded expectations. But they’ve got a lot of work to do.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "I think you could say that we squeezed every drop out of this group.
" The Bruins' 2025-26 season came to a close on Friday night.
(Photo by: Barry Chin/Globe Staff).

title: Bruins’ Nikita Zadorov reveals he was playing through a serious knee injury in playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins "Fully torn off the bone.
" Nikita Zadorov was playing on a torn MCL in the playoffs.

title: Jarren Duran’s 3-run homer brings life back into outfielder, Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox Duran was just looking for an RBI when he stepped into the batter's box in the third inning.
But the home run was a welcome sight.
Jarren Duran got a curveball he could handle and sent it over the right field wall Friday night.

title: Red Sox starting pitcher Jake Bennett offers solid outing in MLB debut vs. Astros
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox “It was just an unreal experience.
… It’s a dream come true.
” 25-year-old Jake Bennett pitched in place of the injured Garrett Crochet Friday.

title: Thousands rally in Boston against billionaires on May Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Local News Labor and immigrant rights groups lead citywide demonstrations calling for economic and workplace reforms.
Mayor Michelle Wu speaks at the May Day rally in Boston.

Courtesy Thousands of demonstrators took part in May Day rallies in Boston on Friday, joining an international day of action focused on workers’ rights, immigration protections, and economic inequality.

title: Here’s ESPN’s latest Red Sox top 10 prospect ranking
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox With former top prospects like Roman Anthony and Marcelo reaching the big leagues, Boston’s farm system rankings look different nowadays.
Left-handed pitcher Payton Tolle is ESPN’s No.
1 Red Sox prospect entering May.

title: Boston Marathon finisher got her tooth chipped by her medal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Marathon “It was just such a crazy incident,” she says.
Alexis Taladay bites her medal after chipping her tooth on it.
Courtesy of Alexis Taladay Unlike most runners, whose marathon experiences culminate in a medal and a celebration after finishing 26.

title: 4 things to know about Patriots 7th-round pick Jam Miller
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots At 5-foot-10, 221 pounds, Miller runs with power and has a low center of gravity.
Jam Miller's first name is actually Jamarion, but he prefers to go by "Jam" for simplicity purposes.
AP Photo/Vasha Hunt The Patriots selected Alabama running back Jam Miller in the 7th round of this year’s draft.
